lostDestinations.source: MONRONeYnews ESC.gov Touch-and-Go Ponca City Airport article
MONRONeYnews ESC.gov Touch-and-Go Ponca City Airport article
MONRONeYnews ESC.gov Touch-and-Go Ponca City Airport article — last service Aug 2006

note: PNC last served Aug 2006; Air Midwest 1979-1981 flew Metroliner IIs to Wichita and one-stop OKC; Frontier/Central also served Kansas City and Dallas earlier decades

lostDestinations.source: Oklahoma.gov Enid Woodring Airport report / web search result: Great Lakes served Enid with EAS subsidy 2005-2006
Web search result: Lone Star Airlines served Enid 1986-1998
Web search result: Frontier served Enid until 1979 deregulation
Web search result: Big Sky Airlines served WDG 1999-2002 on EAS routes

note: EAS-subsidized service; last service Aug 2006 on Great Lakes to DEN and Liberal KS; prior carriers included Metro Airlines 1981-1986, Lone Star 1986-1998, Big Sky 1999-2002, Mesa 2002-2005

lostDestinations.source: Web search result: Central Airlines served Ardmore from ~1951 to 1963
Web search result: Central Airlines served Ardmore with DC-3 service including Dallas routes

lostDestinations.carrier: Lone Star Airlines / Big Sky Airlines
lostDestinations.source: Oklahoma.gov Ardmore Municipal Airport individual airport report

note: Central Airlines served Ardmore from ~1951-1963; later small commuter service continued to DFW before ceasing; airport now general aviation only

lostDestinations.source: Web search result: Continental served Bartlesville 1949-1961 with DC-3s; Central Airlines served 1950-1967
Web search result: Frontier Airlines continued Central service until 1974
Web search result: Frontier continued Central Airlines routes through Bartlesville

note: Commercial service ended ~1974; Continental 1949-1961, Central Airlines 1950-1967, Frontier 1967-1974; now general aviation only

lostDestinations.source: Web search result: Trans-Texas/Texas International served Victoria with SAT nonstops until ~1980
Web search result: Trans-Texas/TI operated DC-3 then jet service DAL-VCT until merger into Continental ~1982
Web search result: Contour Aviation flew VCT to AUS Dec 2014-Oct 2018
Web search result: Sun Air Express EAS service to IAH ended Oct 31 2014

note: Contour Aviation served VCT to AUS/DFW/IAH Dec 2014-Oct 2018; Metro Airlines served as Eastern Express to IAH in 1980s with 7 flights/day; Continental Express/Britt Airways served IAH with EMB-120s

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Frontier served Lawton until 1981
Web search: Continental served Lawton 1948-1975/77
Web search: Metro Airlines served LAW to OKC, Tulsa, IAH starting 1977
Web search: Continental served LAW 1948-1975 with DC-9 jets from 1967

detail: Lawton once had jet service from Continental (DC-9s 1967-1975) and commuter service from Frontier and Metro Airlines; today only American Eagle DFW remains, making it significantly diminished from peak multi-carrier, multi-hub era.
source: Wikipedia: Lawton-Fort Sill Regional Airport; web search results

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Metro Airlines served SWO to OKC 1980-1985
Web search: Exec Express served SWO to TUL with Piper Navajos 1985-1986
Web search: Frontier/Central served SWO on Kansas City-Dallas route until 1979

detail: After 30 years without airline service (1986-2016), Stillwater regained commercial service in August 2016 when American Eagle/Envoy Air began EMB-145 flights to DFW; prior service included Central Airlines DC-3s from 1953 and Frontier jets.
source: Web search: Stillwater Regional Airport Wikipedia / visitstillwater.org history article

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Metro Airlines served SPS to IAH/HOU from 1979
Web search: Metro Airlines served Wichita Falls to IAH from 1979
Web search: ASA Delta Connection served SPS to DFW 1986-2001
Web search: Braniff served Amarillo-Wichita Falls-Dallas route 1930-1969

detail: Braniff served Wichita Falls from 1930 to 1969 when CAB allowed discontinuation; Continental served 1946-1977; Metro Airlines rebranded as American Eagle from 1985; Delta Connection ASA served DFW until 2001; today only American Eagle DFW remains.
source: Wikipedia: Wichita Falls Regional Airport via web search

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Continental Connection Colgan Air Saab 340s ABI-IAH ended 2008
Web search: TI operated up to 4 nonstop DC-9s/day SAT-ABI in late 1970s
Web search: Texas International flew ABI to DAL before merging into Continental ~1982
Web search: Continental served Abilene through 1963

detail: Texas International Airlines operated up to 4 nonstop DC-9s/day from San Antonio to Abilene in the late 1970s; Continental Connection Colgan Air ran IAH service until 2008; today ABI retains DFW nonstops via American Eagle and United Express.
source: Grokipedia / web search: Abilene Regional Airport history

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Continental Connection Colgan Air Saab 340 SJT-IAH ended 2008
Web search: TI DC-9s served SJT-SAT nonstop until merger into Continental ~1982
Web search: Texas International DC-9s served SJT to ELP via Midland until ~1982
Web search: ASA Delta Connection served SJT-DFW until ~1999 when Delta closed DFW hub

detail: San Angelo had extensive multi-destination service from Texas International (DC-9 jets to AUS, ABI, MAF, DFW, IAH, SAT, ELP in 1970), then Delta Connection ASA through ~1999 and Continental Connection until 2008; today only American Eagle DFW remains.
source: Web search: San Angelo Regional Airport Wikipedia history

lostDestinations.source: Web search: United Airlines/United Express ended TYR-IAH service April 2016
Web search: Frontier Airlines served TYR-DEN July 2019-April 2020
Web search: Trans-Texas/TI terminated TYR service 1977
Web search: Delta served TYR on Dallas-Atlanta route until 1956

detail: Tyler lost United Express IAH service in April 2016 and briefly had Frontier DEN nonstops (2019-2020 COVID casualty); previously Delta served TYR on DAL-ATL route until 1956 and Trans-Texas/TI served TYR until 1977; now only American Eagle DFW.
source: Web search: Tyler Pounds Regional Airport Wikipedia / tylertexasonline.com history

lostDestinations.source: Web search: GGG lost IAH service around 2001 when Delta closed DFW hub and regional carriers consolidated
Web search: Trans-Texas operated GGG routes including DAL nonstops through early 1970s
Web search: Rio Airways as Delta Connection served East Texas regional routes including SHV connections

detail: East Texas Regional Airport once served as a Delta Connection hub for Rio Airways with multi-city East Texas service; commercial traffic fell to less than 1% of operations by 2023; today only American Eagle DFW remains.
source: Web search: East Texas Regional Airport Wikipedia; flyggg.com commercial airlines history

lostDestinations.source: Web search: American Eagle SkyWest ended twice-daily DRT-DFW ERJ-145 service April 3 2023
Web search: Contour Aviation codeshare DRT-DFW via BAe Jetstream ended Nov 2017 insufficient ridership
Web search: Lone Star Airlines served DRT to DAL on Saab 340s through the 1990s

detail: Del Rio lost all commercial service on April 3, 2023, when American Eagle/SkyWest ended twice-daily DFW ERJ-145 flights citing soft demand and a nationwide pilot shortage; the city had also lost brief Contour Aviation service in 2017.
source: Web search: Del Rio International Airport Wikipedia history

lostDestinations.source: Paris city FAQ / Wikipedia Cox Field: Exec Express renamed to Lone Star Airlines served PRX to DFW with Swearingen Metroliners until 1997
Web search: Exec Express served PRX 1987-1991 before renaming to Lone Star Airlines

detail: Cox Field last had commercial service in 1997 when Lone Star Airlines ceased DFW Metroliner flights; service began in 1987 under Exec Express before it renamed to Lone Star; the city of Paris confirms no airline service currently exists at Cox Field.

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Rio Airways Delta Connection operated up to 5 DFW flights/day with Beech 99/1900C until 1987 when Rio ceased operations
Web search: Texas International/Trans-Texas operated TPL on HOU-DAL Convair 600 routes until 1976
Web search: Texas International Convair 600 turboprops served TPL to SAT until 1976
Web search: TI/TTa served TPL with 9 Convair 600 flights/day in 1968 including DAL nonstops

detail: Temple's airport had 9 daily Convair 600 flights from Trans-Texas in 1968 including nonstops to SAT and HOU, and five daily DFW Delta Connection Beech 1900 flights in 1985; Rio Airways ceased operations in 1987 ending all service.
source: Web search: Draughon-Miller Central Texas Regional Airport Wikipedia history

lostDestinations.source: Web search: EAS-subsidized service ended March 13 2005 when DOT withdrew subsidy exceeding $200/passenger cap
Web search: Lone Star Airlines served BWD 1987-1998 to DFW on Piper Navajo and Metroliners
Web search: Eagle Commuter Airlines served BWD 1976-1986 to DFW, SJT, AUS, SAT, HOU
Web search: Eagle Commuter Airlines served BWD 1976-1986
Web search: TI Convair 600s served BWD including DFW/DAL nonstops until 1974

detail: EAS subsidy was terminated March 13, 2005 because Brownwood is only 145 miles from Austin, violating the $200/passenger cap rule for cities within 210 miles of a medium hub; last carrier was Mesa/Air Midwest; no service since 2005.
source: Web search: Brownwood Regional Airport Wikipedia / FAA EAS subsidy rules

lostDestinations.source: Web search: Houston Metro Airlines DHC-6 Twin Otter flights GLS-IAH mid-1980s; Texas Airlines commuter service ended ~1990
Web search: Delta mainline jet service BPT/GLS ended 1979
Web search: TTa Convair 600s flew GLS nonstop to HOU and BPT and direct to DAL and AUS until 1972
Web search: TTa served GLS with direct DAL service until ~1972

detail: Galveston had mainline jet service from Delta until 1979 and Braniff service to Houston from the 1930s-1953; final commercial service by small commuter carriers ended around 1990; the island city's proximity to Houston IAH/HOU made service economics impossible.
source: Web search: Scholes International Airport at Galveston Wikipedia / Grokipedia
